note_data.title: Vectors
keyPoints: A **vector** has both magnitude and direction, represented as a column vector or using i, j notation
The **magnitude** of vector a is written as |a| and calculated using Pythagoras' theorem
**Position vectors** describe the position of a point relative to the origin O
Vector **addition** follows the triangle rule: a + b = b + a (commutative property)
**Scalar multiplication** ka multiplies the magnitude by |k| and reverses direction if k < 0
Two vectors are **parallel** if one is a scalar multiple of the other: a = kb
The **zero vector** 0 has magnitude zero and undefined direction
Vector **subtraction** a - b represents the vector from point B to point A
**Unit vectors** have magnitude 1 and show direction only

definitions.definition: A quantity that has both magnitude and direction
The length or size of a vector, always positive or zero
A vector that describes the position of a point relative to a fixed origin
A vector with magnitude 1 that indicates direction only
Vectors that have the same or opposite direction, where one is a scalar multiple of another

howToAnswer.instruction: Calculate the exact value showing all working steps
Prove the given result by clear mathematical steps
Give the answer directly without working or explanation

modelAnswer.question: Given that a = (3, -1) and b = (-2, 4), find |a + b| and state whether vectors a and 2a - 6b are parallel.
markBreakdown: Mark 4: Correct conclusion that vectors are not parallel

commonMistakes: Never write vectors without brackets - always use (x, y) or column vector notation
Students often forget to take the square root when finding magnitude
Writing |a| as negative - magnitude is always positive or zero
Confusing vector addition with scalar multiplication
Not showing that vectors are parallel by finding the scalar multiple k
examinerTips: Vector questions appear in every Paper 2 - usually worth 8-12 marks total
Always draw a clear diagram showing vectors as directed line segments
Show all steps when calculating magnitude using Pythagoras
For parallel vectors, always find the value of k where a = kb
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Vector triangle showing addition a + b with vectors drawn as arrows from tail to head, forming a closed triangle. Include coordinate axes and label all vectors clearly.
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (5 marks) — they asked students to write down the position vector of a ship t hours after it starts sailing, requiring understanding of time-dependent position vectors
This topic appeared in O/N 2022 P1 Q8 (4 marks) — students had to find values of a and b in a vector equation, testing algebraic manipulation of vector components
This topic was examined in M/J 2024 P2 Q6 (6 marks) — candidates needed to write a velocity vector in the form xi + yj and use vector methods to prove collinearity of three points
This topic featured in O/N 2023 P2 Q5 (4 marks) — students were required to find position vectors at specific times and demonstrate understanding of vector addition

workedExample.question: Ship A starts from point O and travels with velocity (3i + 4j) km/h. Ship B starts from point P with position vector (12i + 5j) km and travels with velocity (2i - j) km/h. Find when the ships are closest together. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Write position vectors at time t. Ship A: r₁ = t(3i + 4j) = 3ti + 4tj. Ship B: r₂ = (12i + 5j) + t(2i - j) = (12 + 2t)i + (5 - t)j
Step 2: Find vector AB = r₂ - r₁ = (12 + 2t - 3t)i + (5 - t - 4t)j = (12 - t)i + (5 - 5t)j
Step 3: Distance squared d² = |AB|² = (12 - t)² + (5 - 5t)² = (12 - t)² + 25(1 - t)²
Step 5: For minimum distance, differentiate: d(d²)/dt = 52t - 74 = 0, so t = 74/52 = 37/26 hours
workedExample.answer: The ships are closest together after 37/26 hours (approximately 1.42 hours)
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for each position vector, 1 mark for AB vector, 1 mark for distance squared expression, 1 mark for differentiation, 1 mark for solving

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused position vectors with displacement vectors' — always check whether the question asks for position from origin or displacement between two points
Examiner Report 2024: 'Students frequently omitted unit vectors i and j in their final answers' — always express vector answers in component form with proper notation
Examiner Report 2022: 'Common error was treating vectors as scalars when solving simultaneous equations' — remember to equate corresponding components separately when solving vector equations
note_data.keyDiagram: Essential vector diagram should show: coordinate axes with unit vectors i and j clearly labeled, origin O marked, position vectors drawn as arrows from origin to points, displacement vectors shown between points (not from origin), velocity vectors drawn with appropriate direction and labeled with components. Include time parameter t in position equations when showing motion.
note_data.quickRecap: Vectors have magnitude and direction, expressed as ai + bj. Position vectors start from origin, displacement vectors connect two points. To find unknown components, equate corresponding i and j parts separately. For motion problems, position = initial position + velocity × time. Collinear points satisfy r₂ = r₁ + λ(r₃ - r₁).
